name: Guard Tower
prompt:
Vietnam War firebase guard/observation tower. Elevated fighting position for perimeter defense.

Physical structure:
- Four vertical post legs: wooden poles (~0.15m diameter cylinders), ~6m tall
  - Legs slightly splayed outward for stability (wider at base than top)
  - Spacing: ~2m x 2m at top, ~2.5m x 2.5m at base
- Cross-bracing: diagonal wooden beams between legs on each face (X-pattern)
  - At least 2 levels of cross-bracing
- Platform: wooden floor at top (~5.5m height), ~2.2m x 2.2m
  - Floor planks (flat boxes)
- Railing walls: sandbag walls on all 4 sides of platform, ~1m tall
  - Waist-high protection for standing soldier
- Roof: corrugated tin/metal, slightly pitched (front-to-back slope)
  - Extends ~0.3m beyond platform edges for rain protection
- Ladder: simple wooden ladder from ground to platform, on one face
  - Two vertical rails with ~8 horizontal rungs
- Searchlight: mounted on one corner post above roof
  - Cylindrical housing with lens, on small pivot mount

Colors: Weathered wood (0x8B7355), darker aged wood (0x6B5B3F), tin roof (0x888888), sandbags (0xc2a878), searchlight (0xaaaaaa), light lens (0xffffcc with emissive)
Orientation: Standing on ground, front face toward +Z
Scale: Real-world meters (~6m tall, ~2.2m platform width)
animations: Searchlight sweep: slow rotation track on searchlight pivot, Y axis, sweeping ~60 degrees back and forth over 4 seconds
